title: Beach TV Florida & Alabama — live segment
text: The cannon firing that we do every afternoon at 4 o'clock here at the Grand when our owner decided that we needed a daily tradition and a way to commemorate our historical past here at the hotel. The tribute to the cannon now is so nice because our reenactors dressed in period costume back to the era of when the cannon was built and they do such a nice job of paying tribute to not only our fallen heroes but those who are also still living that have served in our military and all branches of the military are recognized and saluted and paid tribute to. Our kids love it, the grown-ups love it, it's just a great tradition that we started here at the Grand Hotel. We hope to continue that for many years to come. During the World War II, the hotel was closed for business and the owner was able to work with the army and use the hotel as a training facility during World War II. The gentlemen trained and marched out here on the fields, they learned to swim in our swimming pool, they jumped off of the pier and all of those things were in preparation for serving the military during World War II to repair aircraft over in the Pacific. So their service here and training here took them to the Pacific efforts of World War II and they were able to help turn around the events that ultimately facilitated us defeating the Japanese during World War II. The Grand Hotel Marriott is located in Point Clear, Alabama on the eastern shore of Mobile Bay. We're about 13 miles south of Interstate 10 and we hope that when you come to the Grand you will enjoy our peace, tranquility and our southern hospitality. Artists have always been drawn to the sea. Nature, ever changing, stormy to sublime, stirs the creative soul. Justin Gaffrey built his gallery in Blue Mountain many, many years ago. His artwork and the gallery that shelters it are pure originals inspired by the natural beauty of 30A. And so my inspiration comes from within and what surrounds me in this area is a perfect balance for both of us. Justin Gaffrey's gallery is unique. It is a beautiful, welcoming place. Justin welcomes everyone. He derives great joy from first time visitors as they discover, enjoy and appreciate his work. Justin encourages all to view and engage in the essence of the beautiful and welcoming gallery. Justin Gaffrey Gallery is located along 30A at 21 Blue Golf Drive in Blue Mountain Beach. The gallery is open to the public seven days a week, 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Learn more at JustinGaffrey.com. Our fun is back in session at Waterville, USA and we're celebrating 40 years of non-stop thrills on the Gulf Coast and still number one from heart-founding water slides to laid-back floating on the Lazy River plus family favorites like Shrimp boat Village and the Flow Rider. And off the water, check out top golf swing suites, squash and strike bowling, go-karts, mini golf and the Lagoon Bar. What are you waiting for? Celebrate 40 years of fun at the Gulf Coast, best water park, Waterville, USA in Gulf Shores. Hey there, I'm Shane O. You may have seen me on the Travel Channel or not, but here in Destin, you're more likely to see me surfing right here at my favorite home break. When there's waves, I grew up on this beach. Back then, if I was bored, I'd fish, if I was lucky, I'd surf, and if I was hungry, I went to the back porch. Destin's changed a lot over the years, but this is a local staple that's been sitting right here on my favorite beach since 1974. It may have been a little smaller back then, but the flavors haven't changed a bit. It still has the old school atmosphere, friendly service, and fresh golf seafood. It's simple. If you want great golf seafood right on the beach, it doesn't get any better than this. The bottom line is, if you haven't been to the back porch seafood and oyster house, then you haven't been to Destin. Now be sure to check out my top six reasons to visit the back porch video at thebackporch.com. Sunshine Shuttle in Limousine, the largest and most respected ground transportation company in Northwest Florida, now has a convenient kiosk inside the Northwest Florida Beach's airport. Guests may book rides to any destination on the Emerald Coast from the air-conditioned comfort of the terminal. Sunshine Shuttle in Limousine delivers the same high level of customer service experienced travelers have come to expect at world-class resorts. Immaculate smoke-free vehicles and sophisticated technology, which allows Sunshine to track all vehicles and flights in real time. No matter what time your flight arrives, you and your family will have a car and a friendly driver there to greet you. Sunshine provides multiple airport options for group events and special occasions. Want to celebrate by upgrading to a limousine or town car? Sunshine has you covered. Need a custom van for a night on the town? Sunshine is on it. Here for a large convention, book one of Sunshine's many motor coaches and travel together. Sunshine Shuttle in Limousine's unique presence at Northwest Florida Beach's airport once again proves their commitment to consistent customer service, pricing, and professionalism. And like the airport, Sunshine Shuttle in Limousine is always in transit. Hi, I'm Diane Frazier and I'm the executive director at the Emerald Coast Science Center. The Science Center is a 501C3 nonprofit and our mission is to grow and engage a scientifically literate community. We have over 50 hands-on exhibits and what I'd like to do is introduce you to some of our more popular ones. In this area we have hands-on activities that we change weekly. The Makerspace is a brand new trend and science center across the United States and we have come full force into this. We have challenges that change weekly. We also have some of our techie ones to demonstrate today. These are cubelets. Each one of these cubes has a different command and when you put them together you can customize and build your own robot. And these are ozobots. Ozobots have an optical sensor and you draw a line and then they follow your commands based on however you draw your line. Now we're in our animal room and I'd like to introduce you to Avogadro. He is a ball python named after Emilio Avogadro, a famous 16th century Italian chemist. All of the animals that we have here have been donated to us and all of them are named after scientists. So come on down, learn a little bit about some of these great animals and learn about some scientists you might not have been introduced to before because that's who they're named after. For more information please visit our website www.ecscience.org and then come on down and see for yourself all the wonderful opportunities that we have for you to re-engage your curiosity with science and the world around you at the Emerald Coast Science Center in Fort Walton Beach. The tribute to the cannon now is so nice because our re-enactors dressed in period costume back to the era of when the cannon was built and they do such a nice job of paying tribute to not only our fallen heroes but those who are also still living that have served in our military and all branches of the military are recognized and saluted and paid tribute to. Our kids love it, the grown-ups love it, it's just a great tradition that we started here at the Grand Hotel and we hope to continue that for many years to come. During the World War II the hotel was closed for business and the owner was able to work with the army and use the hotel as a training facility during World War II so the gentlemen trained and marched out here on the fields, they learned to swim in our swimming pool, they jumped off of the pier and all of those things were in preparation for serving the the military during World War II to repair aircraft over in the Pacific so their service here and training here took them to the Pacific efforts of World War II and they were able to help turn around the events that ultimately facilitate us defeating the Japanese during World War II. The Grand Hotel Marriott is located in Point Clair, Alabama on the eastern shore of Mobile Bay. We're about 13 miles south of Interstate 10 and we hope that when you come to the Grand you will enjoy our peace tranquility and our southern hospitality. What is a beautiful night here on the Pensacola Sound? I'm here at Flounders with Jason, he's the manager here. Jason this is such a fun place I love that I can come just put my toes in the sand, have a delicious drink, this is the diesel fuel right? Yes ma'am it definitely is. That sounds dangerous a little bit. It is, it's pretty lethal. It's a party in a glass for sure. It tastes very good though I have to admit. So tell me a little bit about this place. You guys have been here a long time. Yes 1980 starting from where you're currently standing everything was just sand and we had a little tiny restaurant inside and we've since gone to you know two acres of just fun and chaos really. Fun and chaos yeah it can't be that. Well tell me a little bit more about this drink I know this is your signature drink it's absolutely delicious tell me what's in this? That has been the staple vodka rum gin triple sec splash of sour and coke fantastic it's very smooth it's almost too smooth. It really is I feel like it's gonna sneak up on me later. Well you only have you're only allowed to here. That's a good policy I agree with that. So you know I love this place because this is a place you can come in no matter what you can have fun whether you're on a date night girls night with your family this is there's something for everyone here. Yes that's been a huge part of what we have tried to accomplish over these years on this everything stuff for the kids stuff for the adults stuff for the younger adults and the adults who just want to hang out until we close. We have live music every night of the week and volleyball and just something for everybody. Absolutely well no matter what you are looking for this is the place you can find it right here at Flounder so come and check it out we'll be looking forward to seeing you thanks Nathan thank you. Being born here I was so blessed to be born here in this city I love Pensacola another place like it in the land I've been all over but Pensacola is very unique it's on the water it's it's got so much history here my mom and daddy both they met here in Pensacola when they came to Appalachicola from what I gather it was a very small city it was a fishing village though but it was enough to support the family of my grandmother you know two boys and four girls so they heard in Appalachicola that there was another city called Pensacola and it was on the coast they had a lot of Italian fishermen here and Uncle Buster he was older so he found a place on Government Street to open up a a fish market in the grocery store here and that's where they raised their family and my mama met my daddy and good combination daddy was a hard worker and my mama was a very sweet loving woman and I'll tell you Pensacola was one of the finest cities to grow up in that's my humble opinion which doesn't mean anything but I was born in Boston on the street and there's not a better place to be in Pensacola that's just how I feel about it you know of course I got attachments here because I was born here Coastal Art Center is a city of Orange Beach facility when you come into the gallery you'll see fine art they are all local artists we have over a hundred artists here in the main gallery you'll see fine art you'll see two-dimensional and three-dimensional works everything is original we have acrylic oil watercolors all those types of two-dimensional work and we have wood carving we have clay works we have glass and jewelry textile art we have a number of things here we have on-site Alabama's premier hot glass blowing facility you can come in and watch anytime every day you can take classes if you want to we have a clay studio here that you can play in the clay as we say and we have an art studio for kids and we do after school things camps workshops we have a number of art things going on and then we have scattered offerings that depending on availability for our teachers we have mixed media and oil painting and watercolor and drawing we have a number of things in our art studio that you can sign up for it the Coastal Art Center is open every day and you can come in for free there's no charge and get out of the heat enjoy some beautiful works plenty to see and do here probably give yourself an hour and a half two hours to walk through we hope you'll stop by to see us Monday through Friday nine to four and if you need more information give us a call check out our website there's virtually no room left on the ceiling of Maguire's Irish pub in Pensacola at last count there are over one million dollar bills signed by Irishmen of all nationalities there's an old Irish saying that one good turn deserves another so everyone was in agreement that another ceiling was needed now Maguire's Irish pub and Dustin is joining the fun and giving Pensacola a run for the money with over five hundred thousand dollar bills and counting lots of people do come to Maguire's to see the money and they usually have their own autographed bill for posterity but the real reason they're here at Maguire's is to kiss the moose. Okay, kidding. Always plenty of enthusiasm at McGuire's. I love that moose, man. The actual reason people come to McGuire's is the beer. Not just any beer, but the finest ale, porters and stouts brewed right here in their own traditional oak and copper brewery. The beer served in McGuire's is all their own and it's all awesome. Tell you what, that's a really good porter. It's really hard to find it. I moved all over the place. I always come back to Pensacola to drink the McGuire's porter. It's fantastic. Although they take their beer seriously, McGuire started his restaurant and they've never forgotten their roots. So food comes first. Incredibly, this body irreverent, high-spirited pub is truly one of America's great restaurants. Holder of the Golden Spoon Award year after year and a Florida Trend Magazine Hall of Fame member. The McGuire's takes have received the Beef Backers Award by the Beef Industry Council. And their hand cut every day and grilled to perfection over hickory wood fires. You can almost taste the aroma seeping out of the smoker as you make your way up the walk. The burgers are as serious as the steaks. As a matter of fact, they're really steak burgers. Each one contains no less than three-quarter pound of pure ground angus steak. The menu offers every burger imaginable and some you never imagined. One of the most popular side orders of McGuire's is the Senate Bean Soup. The same recipe served in the U.S. Senate. But McGuire's has kept the same price since 1977, only 18 cents. Other favorites include the Emerald Isle Corned Beef and Cabbage and fish and chips battered in McGuire's own Red Ale. There's a lot to experience in McGuire's and with two great locations, there's always a party going on. McGuire's and Destin has a sunset deck with live entertainment. No red-blooded Irishman would miss a view like that. McGuire's in Pensacola has the one and only Rupert O'Tolfe Wine Cellar, one of the largest restaurant display wine cellars in the world. McGuire's has been the recipient of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence every year since 1994. And tours of the wine cellar are gladly given to wine lovers. McGuire and Molly Martin along with their children, Billy, Jim, and Amy own and operate McGuire's. So if you're Irish, come into McGuire's. There's a welcome here for you. They truly love their warm and wonderful pubs. They have a unique philosophy which states with firm authority that McGuire's Irish pub is dedicated to warm and friendly service to their guests by employees who give a damn. They pledge to provide the best products at the best value. And if they're not proud of it, they won't serve it. This commitment is as genuine as the good times that have made McGuire's famous. No one leaves McGuire's hungry, and it is all remarkable from the ridiculous to the serious. McGuire's Irish pub is one of America's great steakhouses. The sign above the door says feasting in library hand buttery. And that pretty much sums it up. Irish pub! McGuire's Irish pub. Feasting at the foot of Destin Bridge. Hand botching in Pensacola. McGuire's at 600 East Gregory Street between Bay Bridge and the Civic Center. Open 11 a.m. daily. Visit our gift shop. Check out our website for nightly events. And don't forget your sense of humor. Hi, I'm Tom Elke, but most of you probably remember me as the grouchy old candy maker. Now I'd like to invite you to come in to Florida's largest candy store for a tour. When you walk through the door, the first thing you see is all this beautiful homemade candy that we make right here in the store. Behind me, the candy makers are making all these fresh chocolates that we sell here in the store every day. And we use the finest ingredients, the finest chocolates, finest nuts. And we take a lot of care and a lot of pride in everything we do here at the candy maker. Back here we have this big observation window where the customers could come back and actually look into this big working kitchen and watch the candy being made. You can't go anywhere and find a jelly bean that we don't have. These two customers just finish filling their cups with frozen yogurt from our self-serve frozen yogurt line. But we're using a line of frozen yogurt that's low fat, all natural, all natural ingredients, all natural flavorings. And I never thought I'd see the day when I'd be selling something that was good for you. We also have a complete coffee shop here in the candy store using all Italian coffees and Italian equipment. Using the best, we always use the best, best beans, best of everything. This part of the store opens early in the morning so a person can come down, sit on the bikini deck, look how it over the water. I have a cappuccino or a espresso or a cold drink or even an iced tea. Well there you have it, you've had a tour of what we're calling Florida's largest candy store. We're located just east of the Backport restaurant. We're on the corner. It's a three-story, bright red building. You can't possibly miss it. If you want to take a look at one of the reasons there are so many outstanding restaurants in Destin, you don't have to go far. Visit the Destin Ice Seafood Market in Delhi. They have been supplying the area's finest restaurants for years. And with their newly expanded deli, they are also supplying Destin's finest homes with mouth-watering recipes. Old-fashioned shrimp salad, deviled crab, seafood gumbo, twice-baked potatoes, and so much more. Everything is homemade at Destin Ice. The salads, the entrees, the smoked salmon and tuna dips, plus party trays are available on special order. There's even a bakery where Destin Ice bakes breads and creates confectionery magic. They also carry Mrs. Dean's cakes from Andaluja, Alabama, and Kenny's key lime pies. Destin's favorite seafood market has an extensive specialty meat department. Destin Ice meats are exclusively from Buckhead beef and halprons, rack of lamb, pork chops, whole tenderloins, filets, rib eyes, New York strips and porterhouse steaks. Plus they carry new skis, apple smoked bacon. Although much of the food at Destin Ice is ready to heat and eat, some items are better when finished in your kitchen and complete instructions are provided. One of the most popular ways to enjoy fresh Destin seafood is to prepare the main fish or shellfish course and let the Destin Ice seafood deli do the hard work of preparing hors d'oeuvres and salads for your guests. For example, pick up a few crab cakes, a crisp salad, and a key lime pie. Select two to three pounds of fresh shrimp from the market and deliver a feast of barbecue shrimp with a fast and favorite Destin Ice recipe. Mix one package of River Road barbecue shrimp seasoning mix. Two sticks of unsalted melted butter and three tablespoons of Worcestershire sauce and a shallow baking dish. Place two to three pounds of shrimp with heads on and bake. Place in a 350 degree oven for 20 minutes or until pink. Serve with a loaf of French bread for dipping. Destin Ice has an impressive wine collection as well as many recipes, cookbooks, condiments and spices including Chef Tim Creehan's Grill Plus. They also have a complete line of frozen products, king and snow crab, crab meat stuffing, kong, calamari, mussels, clams, ravioli, alligator, stuffed boneless chicken, turducken, and turducken breast. The staff is friendly and eager to help with any questions you may have about seafood, including how to pack and ship fresh seafood back home. They pack for car and air travel. The Destin Ice Seafood Market Deli and Specialty Meats is located on Highway 98 between Chevron and the Beach Community Bank and opens seven days per week year round. Recipes you have seen on this program and many others are available at Destin Ice. Call 837-8333 for information. When you live on the Gulf of Mexico, you have no good seafood. Here it's a quickly seafood steamer. It's our finest kind. A mountain of fresh shrimp, Alaskan king, dungeonous and snow crab, mussels, clams, smoked sausage, new potatoes, and corn on the cob. Steam to perfection. Not that hungry, no worries. We have baskets, boils, and bites for every appetite. And while you're digging in, catch a game on one of our 64 HDTVs. At O'Quickly's, it's not just dinner. It's a feast that's turned into a tradition for many visiting families. A big taste of the ocean, piled high and priced just right. Wow. And here it is, the finest kind. 12 pounds of succulent seafood. Come hungry, leave happy. O'Quickly's, Destin's seafood and sports place to be. Right off the Emerald Coast Parkway behind the Olive Garden in the Best Buy Shopping Center. In Papillote, one of the many extraordinary dishes of Louisiana, La Nyap. People just seem to love that fish in the bag. It's a fresh filet of grouper with a fresh crab and lump shrimp stuffing and served with a garlic butter brblanc and a Creole twice baked potato. We ran it about 20 years ago, it became so popular, we just decided to stay with it. Customers just really like the hushed puppies because they're made here in-house and we cook it fresh. True to their Louisiana roots, both Louisiana La Nyap restaurants make their homes on the water. Real, working harbors where fishing boats come and go all hours of the day and night. The views are spectacular, providing ever-changing, beautiful backdrops from memorable meals shared together at some of the most sought after tables on the Gulf Coast. For over 30 years, the original Louisiana La Nyap has been a Destin family vacation tradition for thousands of loyal customers, which is pretty remarkable when you consider it's practically hidden from view at the far east end of Destin Harbor on the docks of Sandpiper Cove. It's a little off the beaten path, but once you get there, it's easy to understand why the Louisiana La Nyap is consistently ranked as one of the top restaurants on the Gulf Coast. All the awards and recognition are nice, but Louisiana culture demands you can't take yourself too seriously. Time at the table with your family is meant to be fun, relaxing and unforgettable. The only thing serious around here is the focus on the quality of the food and the warmth and expertise at the service. We want our guests to feel comfortable when they arrive. Dress is casual, you don't have to dress up, we will. We've got fine linen on the table, glassware, kids are welcome. We've got a fairly extensive kids menu. It's gonna be a festive dining room. It's not stuffy, it's not quiet. You're gonna have a good time. We've got a great view of the Destin Harbor. The sunsets are second to none here. We face directly west, so we get one every night. If you miss it tonight, it'll happen again tomorrow. There are a lot of things to love about Louisiana LandYap, so stop by and see what makes us so special. Louisiana LandYap, winner of the Trip Advisor Award of Excellence every year, winner of the Best of the Emerald Coast Award and one of the top local recommendations in Destin and Orange Beach. Open 5 p.m. nightly, year-round in both locations and brunch 10 to 2 on Sundays in Orange Beach. My name is Justin Gaffrey, I'm an artist and I specialize in heavy texture acrylics and I also manufacture heavy texture acrylics here in my studio. What's different about the paint that I make and I work with, it's sculptable. Like I can sculpt it with this and it's just like super thick and it holds the peaks and so when I use a palette knife with it, it holds any shape I want and I've kind of pushed the boundaries of working with extreme textures and some of my paintings can be up to six inches thick. I've developed this paint to just flow off the knife perfectly and it just flows right in, glides off of there and it holds every stroke I wanted to and I've never been able to find a paint that I could buy like that. This paint is for the professional artist all the way down to somebody that just wants to put their hands in it and try it out and I've created ways and shown people through videos of how they can start in a very simple way to achieve a successful painting all the way up to a professional artist working in traditional and pasto techniques. As along with our textures, we do sell like regular viscosity paints that traditional artists use because that's what I use in all the backgrounds of my paints as well. So at Gaffrey Art Material, we provide kits that allow people to have a way to achieve a successful painting and it may be a seascape painting or impressionistic painting or a still life painting. So these kits, when I put them together on the website, we have videos that go along with them, especially for the non-professional and that's what I do. You know, that's how I show people how to paint is get that successful thing that makes you eager to do the next one. So I have two websites. I have gaffreyarmaterial.com and I have justingaffrey.com. Gaffrey Art Material is to buy art supplies and justingaffrey.com is where the gallery of all my works are on top of the gallery in Blue Mountain Beach. My name is Tara Throgmorton. I'm a senior aquarist here at Gullfaring Marine Adventure Park and I specialize in sea turtle rehabilitation. Unfortunately, we have to rescue turtles consistently throughout the entire year.
